preamble: The transformation of the high front rounded vowel [y] (or the palatal glide [j]) into the voiced alveolar stop [d] is a form of fortition and dentalization, documented in historical sound changes where glides harden into full stops.

examples.note: Diachronic shifts in Middle Persian where historical palatal segments fortified into dental stops in specific phonetic environments.
Development of initial palatal glides into voiced palatal or dental stops in specific Himalayan dialects.

sources: Phonological research on Iranian and Indo-Aryan fortition.
